title: Maithili Alphabets – Devanagari Alphabets
content: Maithili is written in the Devanagari script (same as Hindi). Learn vowels (स्वर) and consonants (व्यंजन) below.
intro: Maithili uses the Devanagari script, which is also used for Hindi, Marathi, and Nepali. Below are the vowels and consonants with transliteration and example words.

tables.heading: Example sentences using the alphabet
rows: हम अहाँ सँ प्रेम करैत छी

title: Vowels, Consonants in Maithili and their pronunciation
content: Pronunciation of Maithili vowels and consonants. Maithili has eight vowel phonemes; length can be distinctive in Sanskrit-derived words.
intro: Each letter in Maithili Devanagari represents a consistent sound. Vowels can be short or long; nasalization is common. Use the speaker to hear pronunciation.

blocks.heading: Vowel pronunciation
blocks.content: Front: इ (i), ई (ī). Central: अ (a), आ (ā). Back: उ (u), ऊ (ū), ओ (o), ए (e). In native Maithili words, vowel length is often not phonemic; in Sanskrit-derived words it can change meaning (e.g. दिन 'day' vs दीन 'destitute').

blocks.heading: Consonant pronunciation notes
rows: की अहाँ हमर मददि क' सकैत छी?
Ki aanha hamar madadi ka sakaichhi?
हम अहांके याद क रहल छी
Hum aahānke yād ka rahal chhi

title: Maithili Barakhadi: Symbols for vowels with consonants
content: How vowels combine with consonants in Maithili Devanagari. Vowel signs (matras) are attached to consonant symbols.
intro: In Devanagari, the vowel अ (a) is inherent in a consonant; other vowels are shown with matras (e.g. ा for आ, ि for इ, ी for ई, ु for उ, ू for ऊ, े for ए, ै for ऐ, ो for ओ, ौ for औ).

blocks.heading: Matra examples
blocks.content: क + आ = का (kā); क + इ = कि (ki); क + ई = की (kī); क + उ = कु (ku); क + ऊ = कू (kū); क + ए = के (ke); क + ऐ = कै (kai); क + ओ = को (ko); क + औ = कौ (kau). Same pattern applies to all consonants.

blocks.heading: Words and sentences using vowel signs
rows: अहाँ हमरा सा मैथिली मे बाजू
Aanha hamrā sā maithili me bājū
How do you say ... in Maithili?

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Maithili (Devanagari)
content: Common pronunciation pitfalls when reading Maithili in Devanagari. Maithili has some sounds that differ from Hindi.
intro: Learners familiar with Hindi may notice differences in vowel length, nasalization, and a few consonant sounds. Practice with the sentences below.

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.content: Maithili has two varieties of /e/, /o/, /ai/ and /au/ (long and short) that are not always distinguished in the script. Nasalization (चाँद, काँच) is common. The grapheme ऐ can represent /ai/, /æ/, or /aĕ/ depending on the word.

blocks.heading: Practice sentences
rows: अहाँ अंगरेजी मे बाज सकैत छी?
Is there someone who speaks English?
एतय केओ छथि जे अंगरेजी बजब जानैत छथि?
Etay keo chhathi je angreji bajab jānait chhathi?
शंका गृह केर जानकारी देल जाओ
Shankā grih ker jānkāri del jao

title: Pronunciation of Anusvar (अनुस्वार) in Maithili
content: The anusvar (ं) is a dot above the letter indicating nasalization. In Maithili it can represent ङ, ञ, ण, न, or म depending on the following consonant.
intro: अं is written as a dot (ं) above the consonant. It nasalizes the vowel and often assimilates to the place of the next consonant (e.g. अंग 'body', संग 'with').

blocks.heading: Words and sentences with Anusvar
rows: I will stay for one night
बड्ड दिन भगेल आन्हा के देखला
Badd din bhagel ānhā ke dekhalā

title: Combining consonants in Maithili (मैथिली जोडाक्षरे)
content: Consonant clusters (जोडाक्षर) in Maithili. The halant (्) removes the inherent 'a' so two consonants can join.
intro: When two consonants come together, the first is often written with a halant (्) so that no vowel sits between them (e.g. क् + त = क्त).

blocks.heading: Sentences using consonant combinations
rows: मदद देल जाओ, आवश्यकता मे छी
Madad del jao, āvaśyaktā me chhi
हम चोट सा जख्मी भ गेल छी
Hum choṭ sā jakhmī bha gel chhi
अहाँ केर आज्ञा सा अहाँ केर फोन उपयोग करय चाहब?
Aanhak er ājñā sā aanhak er phone upyog karay chāhab?
I want to talk to a lawyer
हम एकटा वकील सा बात करब चाहै छी
Hum ekṭā vakīl sā bāt karab chāhait chhi

title: Combining consonants with र (r) – Maithili जोडाक्षरे Part 2
content: How र (r) combines with other consonants: as र् before a consonant (reph) or as a subscript (e.g. क्र, प्र, त्र).
intro: र can appear above the line (reph) in clusters like र्क, र्म, or as a subscript in क्र (kra), प्र (pra), त्र (tra), etc.

blocks.heading: Sentences with र clusters
rows: Can you show me on the map?

title: Combining consonants with ह (h) – Maithili जोडाक्षरे Part 3
content: Combinations with ह (h): ध, भ, झ, घ, etc., and conjuncts like ह्न, ह्म, ह्र.
intro: ह appears in many conjuncts (e.g. क्ष, which is क्+ष, and words like प्रेम, धन्यवाद). Aspirated consonants (ख, घ, छ, झ, थ, ध, फ, भ) use a puff of breath.

blocks.heading: Sentences with ह and aspirates
rows: हम अहाँ सँ प्रेम करैत छी
हम अहांके याद क रहल छी
Hum aahānke yād ka rahal chhi
एक गोट भाषा काफी नै होइत
Ek goṭ bhāshā kāphī nai hoit

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants in Maithili
content: Special conjuncts in Devanagari: क्ष (kṣa), त्र (tra), ज्ञ (jña). These have their own shapes in Maithili.
intro: Some consonant clusters have dedicated symbols: क्ष (ksha), त्र (tra), ज्ञ (jña). They are used in Sanskrit-derived and common Maithili words.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Maithili
content: Personal and demonstrative pronouns in Maithili. Maithili distinguishes animate/inanimate in third person.
intro: First person: हम (I), हम सभ (we). Second: अहाँ (you). Third: ओ (he/she), ओ सभ (they). Demonstratives: ई (this), से/ओ (that).

title: Using plurals to indicate respect in Maithili
content: Plural forms used for respect (honorific). Maithili uses सभ, सबहि, and other markers.
intro: Plural and honorific forms for addressing elders or showing respect.

title: Verbs in Maithili
content: Basic verbs and their use. Maithili distinguishes transitive and intransitive verbs.
intro: Common verb roots: कर (do), खा (eat), जा (go), आ (come), देख (see), बज (speak), पढ़ (read), लिख (write), रुक (stop).

title: Simple Present Tense in Maithili
content: Simple present tense: habitual or general truth. Verb + present agreement (छी, अछि, छथि, etc.).
intro: Present tense uses null marker; agreement varies by person and honorificity (e.g. छी for first person, अछि/छथि for third).

title: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Maithili
content: The verb "to be" (अछि, छी, छथि) in present. Used for identity, location, and description.
intro: Forms: अछि (is/are in some contexts), छी (am/are for first person), छथि (is/are for third).

title: Present Continuous Tense in Maithili
content: Present continuous: action happening now. Verb stem + -ैत + form of अछि/छी/छथि.
intro: Equivalent to "is/are doing". Pattern: subject + verb-ैत + छी/अछि/छथि.

title: Simple Future Tense in Maithili
content: Future tense: -ab- (first/second person), -at- (third). "Will do", "will go".
intro: Future marker -अब-/-अत- with verb. हम जाब (I will go), ओ आत अछि (he will come).

blocks.heading: Simple future sentences
rows: Will you go on a trip?
I will stay for one night

title: Future Continuous Tense in Maithili
content: Future continuous: action that will be ongoing at a future time.
intro: Will be doing. Formed with future of हो (be) or equivalent + verb stem + -ैत.

blocks.heading: Future continuous examples
rows: Tomorrow morning you will be travelling
काल्हि भिन्सर अहाँ यात्रा करैत रहब
Kālhi bhinsar aanha yātrā karait rahab
Next week he will be here
अगिला सप्ताह ओ एतय रहत अछि
Agilā saptāh o etay rahat achhi

title: Simple Past Tense in Maithili – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
content: Simple past for intransitive verbs or verbs without object. Past marker -ल- (-al).
intro: हम गेल (I went), ओ आबि गेल (he came). Intransitive past forms.

blocks.heading: Simple past (no object)
rows: बड्ड दिन भगेल आन्हा के देखला
Badd din bhagel ānhā ke dekhalā

title: Simple Past Tense in Maithili – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
content: Past tense for transitive verbs (with object). Ergative pattern in some contexts.
intro: When subject is marked ergative (केँ, सँ), verb agrees with object. देखल (saw), खेलल (played).

title: Simple Past Tense in Maithili – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
content: Past forms of "to be": was/were. छल, छलाह, छलीह, भ गेल, etc.
intro: Past of अछि/छी: छल (was/were), भ गेल (became/has been).

title: Exceptional Verbs in Maithili which change in past tense form
content: Some verbs have irregular past stems (e.g. go → गेल, come → आबि गेल, do → केल).
intro: Irregular past: जा (go) → गेल; आ (come) → आबि गेल; कर (do) → केल; दे (give) → देल; ल (take) → लेल.

title: Past Continuous Tense in Maithili
content: Past continuous: was/were doing. Verb-ैत + past of be (रहल, छल).
intro: Was eating = खाबैत रहल/छल. Combines continuous marker with past.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Maithili
content: Perfect tenses: have/had/will have done. Uses past participle + auxiliary.
intro: Have done = केल + अछि/छी. Had done = केल + छल. Will have done = केल + रहब.

blocks.heading: Perfect tense examples
rows: ओ सभक विवाह भ गेल अछि
O sabhak vivāh bha gel achhi

title: Learn Prepositions in Maithili
content: Postpositions (case markers): मे (in), पर (on), सँ (from/with), केँ (to), केर (of).
intro: मे = in; पर = on; सँ = from/with; दक = towards; तक = until; लगि = for.

blocks.heading: Preposition / postposition examples
rows: The book is on the table

title: Preposition "TO" in Maithili
content: Ways to say "to": केँ (dative/acc.), दक (allative), तक (terminative), ला/लेल.
intro: To a person: अहाँ केँ. To a place: स्टेशन दक. Until: काल्हि तक.

blocks.heading: "To" in sentences
rows: I want to go to the market

title: Sentences with person or living things as object in Maithili
content: When the object is a person or animate, accusative केँ is often used.
intro: Definite animate object: नेना केँ (the boy), अहाँ केँ (you).

blocks.heading: Person as object
rows: हम ओ सभ केँ निमंत्रण देल
Hum o sabh kẽ nimaṃtraṇ del
ओ सभ हमरा केँ अनुसरण करैत छथि
O sabh hamrā kẽ anusaraṇ karait chhathi
हम अहाँ सँ प्रेम करैत छी

title: Saying My/His/Her in Maithili
content: Possessives: हमर (my), अहाँक (your), ओकर (his/her), हमरा (mine/me).
intro: Genitive: हमर नाम, अहाँक घर, ओकर पोथी. Pronominal forms vary by case.

blocks.heading: My / Your / His / Her
rows: हम अहांके याद क रहल छी
Hum aahānke yād ka rahal chhi

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Maithili
content: Question words: की (what), के (who), कत्ता (where), ककहन (when), केहन (how), कतेक (how much).
intro: की = what; के = who; कत्ता = where; ककहन = when; केहन = how.

blocks.heading: Questions in Maithili
rows: की अहाँ हमर मददि क' सकैत छी?
Ki aanha hamar madadi ka sakaichhi?

title: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Maithili
content: Negation in present: नहि, नै. "I do not" = हम ... नहि करैत छी.
intro: नहि / नै before verb or in predicate. I don't = हम नहि ...

blocks.heading: Negative present
rows: हम मैथिली नहि बजि सकैत छी
हम गाेक मांस नहि खाबैत छी
Hum goek māns nahi khābait chhi

title: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Maithili
content: Past negation: नहि + past form. "I did not" = हम नहि केलहुँ.
intro: नहि + verb in past. He did not speak = ओ नहि बजलाह.

title: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Maithili
content: Future negation: will not = नहि + future form.
intro: I will not go = हम नहि जाब. You will not come = अहाँ नहि आबब.

blocks.heading: Negative future
rows: ओ सभ नहि इंतजार करबत छथि
O sabh nahi intajār karabat chhathi
ओ सभ नहि सहमत होबत छथि
O sabh nahi sahmat hobat chhathi

title: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Maithili
content: Nouns: weak/strong stem; optional gender (माली/मालिनि). Plural: सभ, सबहि, दुनू.
intro: Maithili has limited grammatical number. Plural markers: सभ, गोटा, दू, बहुत.

title: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Maithili
content: Nouns with postpositions: घर मे, टेबुल पर, नेना केर.
intro: Oblique form + postposition. घर → घरेँ, घरक, घर मे.

blocks.heading: Nouns with prepositions
rows: The book is on the table

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings in Maithili
content: Similar postpositions: केर/क/कॆ (of), सँ/सँ꣱ (from/with), मे/मोँ (in).
intro: केर = of; क = of (short); सँ = from, with; मे = in; पर = on.

title: Cases in Maithili (विभक्ति)
content: Case markers: Nominative (∅), Accusative/Dative केँ, Instrumental -एँ/सँ, Genitive केर/क, Locative मे/पर.
intro: Nominative = subject; केँ = object/to; केर = possessive; मे = in; पर = on.

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Maithili
content: Imperative: Come! = आऊ. Go! = जाऊ. Do! = करू. Polite: कृपया आऊ.
intro: Imperative forms for orders, requests. Add कृपया for please.

title: Time related words in Maithili
content: Time vocabulary: now, today, tomorrow, morning, evening, day, week, year.
intro: आइ (today), काल्हि (tomorrow), भिन्सर (morning), सान्झ (evening), राइत (night).

title: Verbs indicating cause of action (Causative verb) in Maithili
content: Causative: to make someone do. पढ़ (read) → पढ़ाब (make read/teach).
intro: First causative often -आ-; second -वा-. कर → कराब (get done), खा → खुवाब (feed).

title: Gender-number agreement rule in Maithili
content: In Maithili, grammatical gender/number agreement is limited. Adjectives may agree optionally.
intro: Optional agreement in adjectives (सुन्दर/सुन्दरी). No verb agreement with gender.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style in Maithili
content: Alternative perfect: using लेल (taken) or गेल (gone) as auxiliary with past participle.
intro: Have done = केल + लेल छी. Had done = केल + लेल छल.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Maithili
content: Perfect continuous: have been doing, had been doing. Past participle + रहल + छी/छल.
intro: Have been eating = खाबैत रहल + छी. Had been going = जाइत रहल + छल.

blocks.heading: Perfect continuous
rows: Since morning I have been waiting
भिन्सर सँ हम इंतजार करैत छी
Bhinsar sa hum intajār karait chhi
For two hours she has been cooking
दू घंटा सँ ओ पकाबैत छथि
Dū ghaṇṭā sa o pakābait chhathi

title: Difference between "no/not" and "don't want" in Maithili
content: नहि/नै = no, not. "Don't want" = नहि चाही, चाहब नहि, or नको (like Hindi नको).
intro: Negation vs refusal. I don't want = हमरा नहि चाही. No thanks = नै, धन्यवाद.

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase in Maithili
content: "Going to" for future intention. जाब लागल, आबब लागल.
intro: I am going to go = हम जाब लागल छी. He is going to come = ओ आबब लागल अछि.

blocks.heading: Going to phrase
rows: Tomorrow I am going to come
ओ सभ इंतजार करब लागल छथि
O sabh intajār karab lāgal chhathi

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase in Maithili
content: Past habit: "used to". Often past tense + habitual context.
intro: I used to go = हम जाइत छलहुँ (or जाबैत छलहुँ). He used to eat = ओ खाबैत छल.

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style in Maithili
content: Older or alternative way to express past habit.
intro: Same meaning with different verb form or particle.

blocks.heading: Used to (old style)
rows: अहाँ देर सँ सुतैत रहैत छल
Aanha der sa sutait rahait chhal
We used to go to school

title: Sentence using "If-Then" in Maithili
content: Conditional: If X then Y. जँ ... तँ, यदि ... त.
intro: If you come, I will go = जँ अहाँ आबब, तँ हम जाब.

blocks.heading: If-Then sentences
rows: If you come, I will go
जँ अहाँ आबब, तँ हम जाब
Jã aanha ābab, tã hum jāb
If it rains, we will stay
जँ बरखा होयत, तँ हम रुकब
Jã barkhā hoyat, tã hum rukab
If he eats, she will eat
जँ ओ खाबत, तँ ओ खाबत
Jã o khābat, tã o khābat
If you want, I will give
जँ अहाँ चाहब, तँ हम देब
Jã aanha chāhab, tã hum deb
If he runs, he will win
जँ ओ दौड़बत, तँ ओ जितब
Jã o dauṛabat, tã o jitab
If she reads, she will know
जँ ओ पढ़बत, तँ ओ जानब
Jã o paṛhabat, tã o jānab
If we go, they will come
जँ हम जाब, तँ ओ सभ आबब
Jã hum jāb, tã o sabh ābab
If you speak, I will listen
जँ अहाँ बजब, तँ हम सुनब
Jã aanha bajab, tã hum sunab
If it is good, I will take
जँ नीक अछि, तँ हम ल'ब
Jã nik achhi, tã hum lab

title: Prepositions with Verbs in Maithili
content: Verbs that take a fixed postposition: लाग (need), चाह (want), सँ (from).
intro: Dependent on verb: जाब लागल (going to go), देखब लागल (going to see).

title: Adjectives in Maithili
content: Adjectives describe nouns. नीक (good), पैघ (big), सुन्दर (beautiful).
intro: Optional agreement: सुन्दर/सुन्दरी. Usually before noun.

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to" in Maithili
content: Ability: सक (can). हम करि सकैत छी (I can do).
intro: सकैत छी / सकत अछि / सकैत छथि. Negative: नहि सकैत.

blocks.heading: Can / be able to
rows: की अहाँ हमर मददि क' सकैत छी?
Ki aanha hamar madadi ka sakaichhi?
की हम ई खा सकैत छी?

title: Using verb To Want/To Need in Maithili
content: Want = चाही, चाहब. Need = जरूरी, आवश्यकता.
intro: हमरा चाही (I want). अहाँ केँ जरूरी (you need).

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen" in Maithili
content: To become = भ होब. To happen = होइत, भ होइत.
intro: He became rich = ओ अमीर भ गेल. What happened? = की भेल?

title: Using "Should" in Maithili
content: Should = चाही, करबाक चाही. Advice or obligation.
intro: You should go = अहाँ केँ जाब चाही. I should eat = हमरा खाब चाही.

blocks.heading: Should
rows: अहाँ मेहनति क' पढ़ाई अवश्य करू
Aanha mehanati ka paṛhāi avaśy karu

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to" in Maithili
content: Must / have to = अवश्य, जरूरी, लागल. Strong obligation.
intro: I must go = हमरा जाब लागल. You have to come = अहाँ केँ आबब जरूरी.

blocks.heading: Must / Have to
rows: ओ सभ केँ इंतजार करब जरूरी
O sabh kẽ intajār karab jarūrī

title: Using phrase "to keep doing" in Maithili
content: Keep doing = करैत रहब, करैत रहत. Continuous action.
intro: I keep eating = हम खाबैत रहब. He kept running = ओ दौड़ैत रहल.

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison in Maithili
content: Comparative: more = बेसी, अधिक. Superlative: most = सभ सँ बेसी.
intro: Bigger = पैघ, बेसी पैघ. Biggest = सभ सँ पैघ.

title: Using verb "To Know" in Maithili
content: To know = जानब, जनैत. हम जनैत छी (I know).
intro: Know vs know how: जानब (know), जानब की कहनि (know how to say).

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we" in Maithili
content: Let = दिअ, देऊ. Shall we = हम ... कियाँ? Suggestion.
intro: Let me go = हमरा जाब दिअ. Shall we eat? = हम खाब कियाँ?

title: Which-That / What-That sentences in Maithili
content: Relative: जे (that/which/who), जेकर (whose). जे बात (the thing that).
intro: The book that I read = ओ पोथी जे हम पढ़लहुँ. What you said = जे अहाँ कहल.

blocks.heading: Which / That / What
rows: ओ घर जत्ता हम रहैत छी
O ghar jattā hum rahait chhi

title: Giving instructions formally in Maithili
content: Formal instructions: कृपया (please), अवश्य (must). Polite imperative.
intro: Please come = कृपया आऊ. You must submit = अहाँ केँ जमा करब अवश्य.

blocks.heading: Formal instructions
rows: अहाँ केँ काल्हि जमा करब चाही
Aanha kẽ kālhi jamā karab chāhī

title: Using "To Like" in Maithili
content: To like = नीक लगैत, पसंद. हमरा नीक लगैत छी (I like).
intro: Object + नीक लगैत. I like you = हमरा अहाँ नीक लगैत छी.

blocks.heading: To like
rows: अहाँ केँ ओ नीक लगैत छी
Aanha kẽ o nik lagait chhi
ओ सभ केँ खेलब नीक लगैत छथि
O sabh kẽ khelab nik lagait chhathi
अहाँ केँ चाह नीक लगैत छी?
Aanha kẽ chāh nik lagait chhi?
अहाँ केँ की नीक लगैत छी?
Aanha kẽ ki nik lagait chhi?
हमरा ई जगह नीक लगैत छी
Hamrā i jagah nik lagait chhi
ओ सभ एक अर्क नीक लगैत छथि
O sabh ek ark nik lagait chhathi

title: Sentences using "would" in Maithili
content: Would = conditional or polite. Often same as past or conditional verb.
intro: I would go = हम जाबत (conditional). Would you like? = अहाँ केँ ... नीक लगैत?

blocks.heading: Would
rows: जँ हम सकैत, तँ हम करबत
Jã hum sakait, tã hum karabat

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know" in Maithili
content: To understand = बुझब. To come to know = जानब, पता लागब.
intro: I understand = हम बुझैत छी. I came to know = हमरा पता लागल.

title: Add a question tag in Maithili
content: Question tags: की? (isn't it?), नहि? (right?).
intro: You are coming, right? = अहाँ आबैत छी, की? It is good, no? = ई नीक अछि, नहि?

title: Using verb "To remember" in Maithili
content: To remember = याद करब, याद रहब. हमरा याद अछि (I remember).
intro: Remember = याद. I don't remember = हमरा याद नहि.

blocks.heading: To remember
rows: हम अहाँ केँ याद करैत छी
Hum aanha kẽ yād karait chhi

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs in Maithili
content: Want/need + verb: चाही + infinitive. हमरा खाब चाही (I want to eat).
intro: I want to go = हमरा जाब चाही. She needs to rest = ओकरा आराम करब चाही.

title: Using "To Want" with other person in Maithili
content: Want someone to do: अहाँ चाहब की ओ आबत (You want him to come).
intro: I want you to come = हमरा चाही की अहाँ आबब.

blocks.heading: Want with person
rows: हमरा चाही की ओ सभ जानब
Hamrā chāhī ki o sabh jānab
ओ सभ केँ चाही की हम मदद करब
O sabh kẽ chāhī ki hum madad karab
Do you want me to go?
अहाँ केँ चाही की हम जाब?
Aanha kẽ chāhī ki hum jāb?
हमरा चाही की अहाँ कोसिस करब
Hamrā chāhī ki aanha kosis karab
ओकरा चाही की हम इंतजार करब
Okrā chāhī ki hum intajār karab
I don't want you to leave
हमरा नहि चाही की अहाँ जाब
Hamrā nahi chāhī ki aanha jāb
ओ सभ केँ चाही की ओ गाब
O sabh kẽ chāhī ki o gāb
What do you want me to do?
अहाँ केँ चाही की हम की करब?
Aanha kẽ chāhī ki hum ki karab?

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Maithili
content: Supposed to = करबाक चाही, होबाक चाही. Expected or intended.
intro: I was supposed to go = हमरा जाबाक छल. You are supposed to come = अहाँ केँ आबबाक चाही.

blocks.heading: Supposed to
rows: What am I supposed to do?
They were supposed to be here
You are not supposed to go
I am supposed to meet him
This is supposed to be good
You were supposed to tell me

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs in Maithili
content: Like + verb: खाब नीक लगैत, पढ़ब नीक लगैत.
intro: I like to eat = हमरा खाब नीक लगैत छी. She likes to read = ओकरा पढ़ब नीक लगैत छथि.

blocks.heading: Like with verbs
rows: अहाँ केँ पढ़ब नीक लगैत छी
Aanha kẽ paṛhab nik lagait chhi
हमरा यात्रा करब नीक लगैत छी
Hamrā yātrā karab nik lagait chhi
ओ सभ केँ खेलब नीक लगैत छथि
O sabh kẽ khelab nik lagait chhathi
हमरा इंतजार करब नीक नहि लगैत
Hamrā intajār karab nik nahi lagait
अहाँ केँ नाचब नीक लगैत छी?
Aanha kẽ nāchab nik lagait chhi?
ओ सभ केँ बजब नीक लगैत छथि
O sabh kẽ bajab nik lagait chhathi

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object in Maithili
content: Verbs where subject takes case and verb agrees with object. देखब (see), सुनब (hear).
intro: I see you = हम अहाँ केँ देखैत छी. He gave me = ओ हमरा केँ देल.

title: Short forms in spoken Maithili
content: Colloquial shortenings: छी → छि, अछि → अइछ. Common in speech.
intro: Written vs spoken: मधु → मउध, अछि → अइछ (some dialects).

title: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Maithili
content: Greetings and good wishes in Maithili.
intro: प्रणाम, स्वागत, धन्यवाद, शुभकामना, बधाई.

blocks.heading: Greetings and wishes
rows: अहाँ सँ मिलि क' खुशी भेल
Aanha sa mili ka khushī bhel

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Maithili
content: Compound verbs: main verb + auxiliary (लाग, दे, ले, पड़). खा लेल, दे देल.
intro: Complete action: खा लेल (ate up), दे देल (gave away), आबि गेल (came).

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Maithili
content: Ways to address: ओहो, अरे, हे, कानि सुनू (excuse me).
intro: Hey! = ओहो, अरे. Sir = महाशय. Madam = महोदया.

title: Exclamations in Maithili
content: Exclamations: अहा!, ओह!, हाय!, वाह!, क्या!.
intro: Wow! = वाह! Oh no! = हाय! Alas! = अहा!

title: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc. in Maithili
content: Polite requests: की हम ... सकैत छी? (May I?). की अहाँ ... सकैत छी? (Can you?).
intro: May I come in? = की हम भीतर आबि सकैत छी? Can you help? = की अहाँ मदद करब सकैत छी?

blocks.heading: Requests
rows: की हम भीतर आबि सकैत छी?
की अहाँ हमर मददि क' सकैत छी?
Ki aanha hamar madadi ka sakaichhi?
की हम एतय बैठि सकैत छी?
की अहाँ हमरा पानी दे सकैत छी?
Ki aanha hamrā pāni de sakaichhi?
की हम अहाँक फोन उपयोग करब सकैत छी?
Ki hum aanhak phone upyog karab sakaichhi?
की अहाँ मीड़ुम कहि सकैत छी?
की अहाँ इंतजार करब सकैत छी?
की हम ई ल' सकैत छी?
की अहाँ हमरा देखा सकैत छी?
की अहाँ ई लिखि सकैत छी?

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might in Maithili
content: Uncertainty: होयत (might), सकत (may). बहुत सकत (maybe).
intro: It may rain = बरखा होयत. He might come = ओ आबत. Maybe = बहुत सकत.

title: Adjectives made from verb in Maithili
content: Verbal adjectives: करैत (doing), केल (done). पढ़ैत नेना (boy who reads).
intro: Present: verb + -ैत. Past: verb + -ल. Reading boy = पढ़ैत नेना.

title: Adjectives related to prepositions in Maithili
content: Adjectives from place/relation: घरक (of house), मे रहैत (living in).
intro: Inside = भीतरक. Outside = बाहिरक. With me = हमरा सँ.

title: Using May to express wish in Maithili
content: Wish: होय (may it be). Long live = जियत रहू. May you be happy = अहाँक खुशी होय.
intro: Blessing: अहाँक मंगल होए. May God bless = भगवान आशीर्वाद देओ.

blocks.heading: May (wish)
rows: May the force be with you

title: Phrase "To Make Someone do something" in Maithili
content: Causative: make someone do = कराब, खुवाब. I made him go = हम ओकरा जाब करवालहुँ.
intro: Make eat = खुवाब. Make read = पढ़ाब. Get something done = कराब.

blocks.heading: Make someone do
rows: हम ओ सभ केँ इंतजार करवाल
Hum o sabh kẽ intajār karavāl

title: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc. in Maithili
content: General: कतबो (somewhere), केओ (someone), किछु (something), कोनो (any).
intro: Somewhere = कतबो. Anyone = कोनो. Something = किछु. Everywhere = हर जगह.

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation in Maithili
content: Have = अछि (is), केर (of). I have = हमर ... अछि. No verb "have"; use possessive + अछि.
intro: I have a book = हमर पोथी अछि. He has a house = ओकर घर अछि.

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Maithili
content: What does X mean? = X के अर्थ की अछि? Meaning = अर्थ, मतलब.
intro: How to ask: ई के अर्थ की अछि? (What does this mean?).

blocks.heading: Meaning
rows: What is the meaning of life?

title: Conjunctions in Maithili – Part 1
content: And = आ, ओ. But = मुदा, किंतु. Because = किएकि. So = तँ. That = जे.
intro: And = आ. But = मुदा. Because = किएकि. So = तँ. Although = हुनकर बावत.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 1
rows: हम जनैत छी जे अहाँ आबल
Hum janait chhi je aanha ābal

title: Conjunctions in Maithili – Part 2
content: As = जकाँ. Or = या. Neither...nor = न ... न. If = जँ. In case = यदि.
intro: As well as = आ. Or = या. If = जँ. Provided = यदि.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 2
rows: जकाँ हम कहैत छी तँ करू
Jakā̃ hum kahait chhi tã karū

title: Conjunctions in Maithili – Part 3
content: After = बाद. Then = तँ. As far as = जतेक. As long as = जतेक समय. As if = जकाँ.
intro: After = बाद. Then = तँ. As if = जकाँ ओ...

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 3
rows: As long as you are here

title: Conjunctions in Maithili – Part 4
content: As soon as = जहिना. Once = एक बेर. Rather than = बजाय. Instead of = बजाय. So that = जाहिर.
intro: As soon as = जहिना. Once = एक बेर. So that = जाहिर.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 4
rows: Whether you like it or not
अहाँ केँ नीक लगय या नहि
Aanha kẽ nik lagay yā nahi

title: List of Conjunctions in Maithili
content: Common conjunctions: आ, मुदा, किएकि, तँ, जे, जँ, या, बाद, जकाँ, जतेक.
intro: Summary list with examples.

title: Combining sentences in Maithili
content: Joining two sentences: जे (that), आ (and), मुदा (but). Relative clause with जे.
intro: Two clauses: हम जानैत छी जे अहाँ आबल (I know that you came).

blocks.heading: Combining sentences
rows: हम जानैत छी जे अहाँ आबल
Hum jānait chhi je aanha ābal
He said that he would go
She is happy because she passed
ओ खुश छथि किएकि ओ पास भेल
O khush chhathi kiekī o pās bhel
She said that she is fine
ओ कहल जे ओ नीक छथि
O kahal je o nik chhathi
हम ओकर आबब तक इंतजार केल
Hum okar ābab tak intajār kel
If you want, I will stay
जँ अहाँ चाहब, तँ हम रुकब
Jã aanha chāhab, tã hum rukab

title: Compound verbs in Maithili
content: Compound verb = main verb + auxiliary (ले, दे, पड़, जा). खा लेल, दे देल.
intro: Conjunct verbs: करि देत (do and give), खा लेत (eat up).

title: Active Passive voice in Maithili
content: Passive: object as subject, verb in passive form. क' लेल (done), देखल (was seen).
intro: Active: ओ पोथी पढ़ल (He read the book). Passive: पोथी पढ़ल गेल (The book was read).

title: Active Passive voice in Maithili – Style 2
content: Passive with य/जा (go): कराब (to be done), देखाब (to be shown).
intro: Infinitive passive: खाब (to eat) → खाब याब (to be eaten).

blocks.heading: Passive style 2
rows: To be or not to be

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think" in Maithili
content: To feel = महसूस करब, लागब. To think = सोचब. हम सोचैत छी (I think).
intro: I feel = हमरा लागैत छी. I think = हम सोचैत छी.

blocks.heading: Feel / Think
rows: We feel that you are right
हमरा लागैत छी जे अहाँ सही छी
Hamrā lagait chhi je aanha sahī chhi

title: Idioms and Phrases in Maithili
content: Common idioms and set phrases in Maithili.
intro: Time is money = समय बहुमूल्य अछि. Where there is a will = जतय इच्छा अछि.

blocks.heading: Idioms and phrases
rows: Where there is a will there is a way
जतय इच्छा अछि ततय रस्ता अछि
Jatay ichchhā achhi tatay rastā achhi
All is well that ends well
जे नीक सँ समाप्त होय से नीक
Je nik sa samāpt hōy se nik
Hit the nail on the head
Let the cat out of the bag
Cost an arm and a leg

title: Idioms and Phrases in Maithili – Part 2
content: More idioms: actions speak louder, easy come easy go, etc.
intro: Actions speak = काज बात सँ बेसी कहैत अछि.

blocks.heading: Idioms part 2
rows: काज बात सँ बेसी कहैत अछि
Kāj bāt sa besī kahait achhi
Every cloud has a silver lining
देरी सँ नीक नहि सँ बेसी
Derī sa nik nahi sa besī
जतय धुआँ अछि ततय आगि अछि
Jatay dhuā̃ achhi tatay āgi achhi
A stitch in time saves nine
समय मे एक कोसिस नौ बचाबैत अछि
Samay me ek kosis nau bachārait achhi

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2 in Maithili
content: Conditional type 2: unreal or hypothetical. जँ ... होत तँ ... होत.
intro: If I had known = जँ हम जानल होत. If you had come = जँ अहाँ आबल होत.

title: List of Body parts in Maithili
content: Body part names in Maithili.
intro: Head = माथा. Eye = आँख. Hand = हाथ.

title: Frequently used words – Miscellaneous in Maithili
content: Common everyday words.
intro: Eat = खाउ. New = नया. Help = सहायता.

title: Relations in Maithili
content: Family and relationship terms.
intro: Father = बाबू. Mother = मां. Brother = भाई.

title: Numbers in Maithili – Part 1
content: Numbers 1 to 20 and basic counting.
intro: One = एक. Two = दू. Three = तीन.

title: Numbers in Maithili – Part 2
content: Numbers 21 to 100 and beyond.
intro: Twenty-one = एक्कीस. Hundred = सय.

title: Fractional numbers, sequence, percentage in Maithili
content: Fractions, order, and percentage.
intro: Half = आधा. Quarter = पाव. Percent = प्रतिशत.

title: Frequently used verbs in Maithili – Part 1
content: Common verbs: to be, to do, to go, to come.
intro: To go = जाब. To come = आब. To do = करब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Maithili – Part 2
content: More common verbs: give, take, want, like.
intro: To give = देब. To take = ल'ब. To want = चाहब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Maithili – Part 3
content: Verbs: work, play, cook, wash, build.
intro: To work = काज करब. To play = खेलब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Maithili – Part 4
content: More verbs: put, keep, change, grow.
intro: To put = राखब. To keep = रखब.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लावब/देब in Maithili
content: देब (to give) and लावब (to apply/bring) with multiple uses.
intro: Give = देब. Apply = लावब. Put on = लावब.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लागब in Maithili
content: लागब: to feel, to need, to stick, to start.
intro: To feel = लागब. To need = लागब. To stick = लागब.

title: List of Maithili Adjectives – Part 1
content: Common adjectives: big, small, good, bad.
intro: Big = नमहर. Small = छोट. Good = नीक.

title: Adverbs in Maithili – Part 1
content: Common adverbs: here, there, now, today.
intro: Here = एतय. There = ओतय. Now = अखन.

title: List of adverbs in Maithili
content: More adverbs: inside, outside, up, down, quickly.
intro: Inside = भीतर. Outside = बाहिर. Quickly = तेजी सँ.

title: List of vegetable names in Maithili
content: Common vegetables in Maithili.
intro: Potato = आलू. Tomato = टमाटर. Onion = प्याज.

title: List of names of fruits in Maithili
content: Fruit names in Maithili.
intro: Mango = आम. Banana = केला. Apple = सेब.

title: List of names of birds in Maithili
content: Bird names in Maithili.
intro: Crow = कौआ. Sparrow = गौरैया. Peacock = मोर.

title: List of names of insects in Maithili
content: Insect names in Maithili.
intro: Ant = किरिल. Bee = मधुमक्खी. Mosquito = मच्छर.

title: List of colours in Maithili
content: Colour names in Maithili.
intro: Red = लाल. Blue = नीला. Green = हरियर.

title: List of names of flowers in Maithili
content: Flower names in Maithili.
intro: Rose = गुलाब. Lotus = कमल. Jasmine = चमेली.

title: List of names of animals in Maithili
content: Animal names in Maithili.
intro: Dog = कुकुर. Cat = बिराली. Cow = गाई.

title: Frequently used or common words in Maithili – Miscellaneous
content: Extra common words for daily use.
intro: Yes = हाँ. No = नहि. Please = कृपया.

title: Diwali Festival in Maithili
content: Diwali greetings and festival phrases.
intro: Happy Diwali = दीपावली को शुभकामना.

blocks.heading: Diwali
rows: Diwali is a festival of lights
दीपावली सँ पहिने घर साफ करू
Dīpāvalī sa pahine ghar sāph karū

title: Simple Sentences in Maithili – Introduction/Salutation
content: Greetings and introductions.
intro: Hello = नमस्कार. My name is... = हमर नाम ... अछि.

blocks.heading: Introduction/Salutation
rows: अहाँ सँ भेट क' खुशी भेल
Aanha sa bhet ka khushī bhel
बड़ दिन भेल अहाँ केँ देखल
Baṛ din bhel aanha kẽ dēkhal
अहाँ हमरा संग मैथिली मे बजू
Aanha hamrā saṃg Maithilī me bajū
How do you say ... in Maithili?
अहाँ ... केन मैथिली मे बजबैत छी?
Aanha ... kēn Maithilī me bajabait chhi?

title: Simple Maithili Sentences – Time Related
